operating_state: Total revenue reached $10.1 billion in 2024, up from $10.0 billion in 2023, driven by Data Centers growth and European expansion. U.S. & Canada property grew modestly on tenant billings from colocations and amendments, offset by straight-line accounting impacts and T-Mobile legacy Sprint lease cancellations. Latin America and Africa & APAC declined from foreign currency headwinds including Nigerian Naira, Ghanaian Cedi, Brazilian Real, and Mexican Peso depreciation totaling $236 million negative impact. Tenant churn remained low at approximately 2% but elevated T-Mobile master lease cancellations expected through 2025.
cost_structure: Operating margins remained high with property operations generating 98% of revenue and minimal incremental costs for adding tenants to existing sites. The company extended estimated useful lives of tower assets from 20 to 30 years effective January 2024, resulting in approximately $730 million decrease in annual depreciation expense. Total depreciation, amortization and accretion declined to $2.0 billion from $2.9 billion in 2023, while operating expenses decreased primarily from lower personnel costs.
material_events: Completed strategic divestiture of ATC TIPL for $2.2 billion in September 2024, recording a $1.2 billion loss primarily from cumulative translation adjustment reclassification, with proceeds used to repay existing debt. Divested Australia and New Zealand subsidiaries for $78 million generating $8.5 million net gain. Issued $3.6 billion in senior notes with maturities from 2029 to 2035 and repaid $12.4 billion in various maturing debt obligations. Added approximately 2,450 communications sites globally through acquisition and construction.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in $1.5 billion negative impact to comprehensive income, with emerging market currencies creating headwinds across Africa, APAC and Latin America. Ghana and Nigeria economies were deemed highly inflationary, requiring adoption of U.S. Dollar functional currency accounting. The company maintains $7.5 billion EUR in outstanding debt creating translation risk exposure, while variable rate debt totaling $2.2 billion creates interest rate sensitivity.
forward_outlook: Management expects nearly $54 billion of non-cancellable tenant lease revenue over future periods based on existing leases, with 2025 total capital expenditures projected at $1.6 to $1.7 billion including approximately 1,950 to 2,550 new communications sites globally. The company maintains purchase options on Verizon and AT&T towers through 2047 and 2032 respectively. Strategic focus remains on increasing occupancy of existing sites, selective portfolio growth, and maintaining investment grade credit ratings with $12.0 billion available liquidity.
uncertainty_profile: Asset retirement obligations totaling $2.4 billion with $4.5 billion in estimated undiscounted future cash outlays carry timing and cost estimation risk across the global portfolio. Debt covenant compliance requires maintaining consolidated total leverage below 6.0x and senior secured leverage below 3.0x, while securitization structures require DSCR maintenance above 1.30x to avoid cash trapping. Unrecognized tax benefits remain subject to resolution across multiple international jurisdictions, while pending OECD implementation may result in material Top-Up Tax if Real Estate Investment Vehicle qualification fails.
